element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Available to all characters from start; foundation for all combat
Introduced alongside melee; powers vary by character (blasts, cards, teleportation, etc.)
Learning to collect pickups and manage 10-point health bar
Different characters unlock different shortcuts and optional content paths; incentivizes character-switching
Risk/reward mechanic: stationary charging for increased damage/AoE; not all characters possess this
Defeat required to proceed; tests learned mechanics
Boss-only stages; significant encounters against story enemies (Apocalypse, Deathbird, etc.)
Unique mechanics: no melee attacks, limitless energy blasts, hovering, electromagnetic orb traversal; drastically changes playstyle

typical_relief_triggers: Narrative breaks via Professor X/Cerebro communication
typical_tension_triggers: Minor boss encounters at stage ends
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Traversal-based tension with punctuated boss encounters. Tension builds through enemy/hazard density, releases via health pickups and character switching flexibility. Nine-life system creates overall rising tension (no continues, game over = restart from beginning).

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Character-specific traversal abilities (wall-climbing, flight, etc.) unlock optional shortcuts and health pickups; certain stage layouts cater to specific characters' strengths

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic mechanics (melee, ranged attacks, health management) with minor boss encounters; character selection flexibility reduces pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Traversal abilities become mandatory for progression; stage layouts increasingly cater to specific character strengths; intermediate boss fights
difficulty_curve.late_game: Major boss-only stages; Magneto unlocked (drastically changes approach options); final confrontation with Phalanx clones of X-Men themselves
notable_spikes: Stage 3 completion: Magneto unlock radically changes available strategies
Major boss fights (Apocalypse, Deathbird) comprising entire stages
Final Nexus and clone factory sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages contain optional paths accessible only to characters with specific abilities (wall-climb, flight, teleportation). Health pickups often placed in these optional areas. Incentivizes replaying same stage with different characters.
Minor bosses appear at end of traversal stages; major bosses comprise entire stages. Narrative progression (Phalanx contamination spreading to new locations) maps to location-based level sequencing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Apocalypse's facility, Savage Land, Phalanx ship, clone factory, Nexus

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Cold open with deferred title sequence
notable_level_patterns.description: Game begins immediately with Stage 1 before title sequence appears. Unusual structure that throws player into action first, establishing mechanics before narrative context.

sources_used: Wikipedia: X-Men 2: Clone Wars (gameplay, characters, plot, mechanics)
